content: State-of-the-art 2024–2025 active learning agent memory systems can be modified to implement a recursive A2A protocol where agents selectively query the episodic memory of other agents as unlabeled external data stores, rather than just querying static human or environment datasets. This turns multi-agent collaboration into a distributed active learning loop where each agent prioritizes which other agent's underutilized episodic memory traces are most valuable to improve its own task performance.
